About.title: About
About.subtitle: Computer Engineer (UFC), Senior Full Stack Developer
About.objectives: Expertise across all project stages, from concept to software and hardware development. I pursue code quality, maximum performance and an enhanced user experience with emphasis on accessibility. My multidisciplinary background allows me to deliver innovative, high-end solutions.
About.summary: I'm a Senior Full Stack Developer graduating in Computer Engineering at UFC, working in development, evolution and maintenance of critical systems. I lead technical and architectural decisions, from concept to deployment, on highly available applications, DevOps practices and resilient architectures.
About.summarySecondary: I have an analytical, multidisciplinary profile, with experience in corporate systems, educational platforms and innovation projects. I'm developing my thesis on real-time systems and teleoperation with low-latency communication, integrating software, hardware and infrastructure, with focus on performance, scalability and reliability.

Thesis.title: Thesis
Thesis.fullTitle: Design and Implementation of a Teleoperated Vehicle with Simulated Transmission and Force Feedback
Thesis.subtitle: Bachelor Thesis in Computer Engineering — Federal University of Ceará
Thesis.coadvisor: Eng. Me. Artur Rodrigues Rocha Neto
Thesis.hypothesis: Plain UDP over a home Wi-Fi network, combined with force feedback computed from an embedded IMU, is sufficient for responsive teleoperation on low-cost hardware.
Thesis.objective: Design, implement and validate a teleoperation architecture for a 1:5-scale Formula 1 vehicle, evaluating latency, packet loss, motor model fidelity, haptic response, autonomy and thermal behavior.
Thesis.motivation: Teleoperation systems are relevant in mining, space exploration and military operations — where latency and haptic feedback determine task precision. Existing commercial solutions cost over R$ 50,000 and are complex. This work demonstrates that equivalent architecture is viable on R$ 1,300 of open hardware.
Thesis.architectureDescription: Three layers: embedded vehicle (Raspberry Pi 4, camera, IMU, motor, servos), PC client (decodes MJPEG video, computes force feedback, shows telemetry) and Logitech G923 simulator. Communication uses three independent UDP ports — video, telemetry and commands — so that video frames don't block the 100 Hz required by haptic feedback.
Thesis.architectureFigure: Three-layer architecture and three independent UDP ports (video, telemetry, commands).
Thesis.prototypeFigure: 1:5-scale F1 prototype used in the 13 experimental sessions.
Thesis.g923Figure: Logitech G923 — 900° wheel, progressive pedals and native force feedback via Linux evdev.
Thesis.zonesFigure: Gear efficiency zones and τbase time constants.
Thesis.latencyFigure: Cumulative distribution of end-to-end latency — p50 at 55.3 ms.
Thesis.stackEmbeddedList: Raspberry Pi 4 (8 GB) · OV5647 5MP CSI camera · BMI160 IMU · DC 775 motor · BTS7960 H-bridge · 3× MG996R servo · PCA9685 · Python
Thesis.stackClientList: Python · OpenCV · Tkinter · evdev · raw UDP
Thesis.stackSimulatorList: Logitech G923 · 900° wheel · Progressive pedals · Paddle shifters · evdev force feedback
Thesis.stackPowerList: Arduino Pro Micro · 2× ACS758 Hall effect · INA219 · 3S LiPo voltage divider
Thesis.modelDescription: DC 775 motor with five-gear transmission modeled as a first-order system G(s) = K / (τ_eff · s + 1), where τ_eff = τ_base(g) · M_zone. τ_base grows from 2 s (1st gear) to 10 s (5th); M_zone is 1.0 (IDEAL), 10.0 (SUBOPTIMAL) or 25.0 (POOR). Operating in the wrong gear for the RPM range penalizes response, mimicking a real transmission.
Thesis.ffDescription: Seven effect types across eight parallel instances: conditional effects (centering, damping, friction, inertia) updated at 1 kHz inside the wheel firmware; lateral force based on BMI160's lateral G at 100 Hz; virtual hard stops at travel limits; and impact vibration synchronized with sensors. Per-effect caching reduced sensor-thread blocking from 12–60 ms to 0–15 ms per packet.
Thesis.experimentsDescription: 13 experimental sessions — 9 indoor (free wheels, 27±2 °C) and 4 outdoor on a 55.81 m track — totaling ~165 min and ~983k aggregated samples.
Thesis.resultsDescription: All targets met. End-to-end latency at 1.1× the goal, with bottleneck on shared Wi-Fi — not on the vehicle. Migrating to a dedicated link would reduce p50 by an order of magnitude with no code changes.
Thesis.conclusion: Plain UDP + first-order model + direct force feedback deliver responsive teleoperation on low-cost hardware. The main limitation was structural, not electronic: PLA chassis and motor mounts gave way on track. The work validates the hypothesis and opens room for evolution with a metal chassis and dedicated network link.
Thesis.referencesDescription: {count} references cited in the thesis, grouped by type. Click each category to expand.

Cookie.title: Cookies and privacy
Cookie.description: I use cookies to understand who visits the portfolio (Google Analytics via GTM). No personal data is collected and consent expires in 180 days.

Privacy.title: Privacy policy
Privacy.intro: This document describes how inaciorodrigues.dev.br processes personal data, in compliance with the Brazilian General Data Protection Law (LGPD, Law 13.709/2018) and aligned with the GDPR principles.
Privacy.section1: Inácio Rodrigues de Matos Galvão. Contact: inaciormgalvao@gmail.com.
Privacy.section2: On visits, anonymous aggregated data (country, browser, device, pages visited) via Google Analytics, Vercel Web Analytics and Vercel Speed Insights. On the contact form, name, email, message and IP address voluntarily provided.
Privacy.section3: Aggregated usage metrics to improve the site (legitimate interest, LGPD art. 7, IX). Direct reply to spontaneous contacts (preliminary procedures initiated by the data subject, art. 7, V).
Privacy.section4: Data may be processed by the following operators: Vercel (hosting, analytics, speed insights), Google (GTM and GA4, only after consent), Cloudflare (Turnstile anti-spam) and Resend (form email delivery). No data is sold.
Privacy.section5: A single functional first-party cookie (portifolio-cookie-consent) stores the LGPD banner choice for 180 days. Google analytics cookies are only set after explicit consent. Vercel and Cloudflare Turnstile do not use tracking cookies.
Privacy.section6: Consent cookie: 180 days. Contact messages: kept for the time needed to respond; deleted on request. Analytics metrics: as per each operator's policy.
Privacy.section7: You may request at any time: confirmation of processing, access, correction, anonymization, blocking, deletion, portability, information about sharing and revocation of consent. Requests are answered within 15 business days.
Privacy.section8: Data protection officer: the controller. To exercise any right, email inaciormgalvao@gmail.com with the subject "LGPD".

Terms.title: Terms of use
Terms.intro: By accessing inaciorodrigues.dev.br you agree to the terms below. They govern the use of public content on this non-commercial professional portfolio.
Terms.section1: Texts, project descriptions and photographs are authored by the controller. The source code is available at github.com/inacio-dev/portfolio under the MIT license. Written content is not covered by the MIT license — if you fork this repo as a template, replace the personal content before publishing.
Terms.section2: Logos and names of companies and institutions (B&Q Energia, NeuroArq Academy, UFC and others) belong to their respective owners and appear only as professional reference, without any sponsorship relationship.
Terms.section3: Content is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. The controller is not responsible for any damages arising from the use of the information or code on this site.
Terms.section4: These terms are governed by Brazilian law. Any disputes not resolved amicably are subject to the courts of Fortaleza/CE, Brazil.
